local_names.romanized_local: Dahi Vada / Dahi Bhalla / Thayir Vadai

food_subgroup: Lentil fritters soaked in spiced sweetened yogurt

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has no specific concept for Dahi Vada. Nearest parent is a lentil-based snack in dairy sauce. A new concept for yogurt-based Indian chaat is recommended.
ontology.foodex2_description: Legume-based fritter in dairy condiment, Indian style (closest parent)

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving of dahi vada is 2 pieces (200g total) — two soft soaked urad dal fritters submerged in chilled sweetened yogurt, topped with tamarind chutney, green chutney, chaat masala, and chilli powder.

nutrient_source.title: Dahi Vada — ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods, Entry EI000020
nutrient_source.organization: Indian Council of Medical Research — National Institute of Nutrition (ICMR-NIN)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Exact match in ICMR-NIN under entry EI000020 — Dahi Vada, standard North Indian preparation.

gi_source: University of Sydney GI Database — estimated from urad dal fritter in yogurt; the yogurt protein and fat, combined with lentil structure, moderates the GI.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium approximately 596mg per 2-piece serving (200g) — elevated; chutney toppings (especially tamarind chutney) and chaat masala are the primary sodium contributors.
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should limit dahi vada to 1 piece and request reduced-salt chutneys. The yogurt component provides calcium which is beneficial for blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate GI (~52); glycaemic load per serving is approximately 19. The sweetened yogurt contributes 6.5g sugar per 100g which is a concern for diabetic patients.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Diabetic patients should request dahi vada with unsweetened yogurt (no added sugar) and minimal tamarind chutney. Limit to 1 piece. The lentil base provides good protein content.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Urad dal is an excellent source of folate and protein; yogurt provides probiotics and calcium; cumin and chilli have anti-inflammatory properties.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Suitable for patients with rheumatoid arthritis when made with unsweetened yogurt. The probiotic content of fresh yogurt and the anti-inflammatory spice toppings are beneficial.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Dahi Vada is a popular Indian chaat dish — urad dal fritters (vadas) that are first deep-fried, then soaked in water to remove excess oil and soften, then submerged in chilled sweetened plain yogurt and finished with a generous topping of dark tamarind chutney, vibrant green mint-coriander chutney, chaat masala, roasted cumin, and chilli powder. Served cold, it is simultaneously creamy, tangy, sweet, and spiced. The deep-fried vada absorbs the yogurt to become soft and spongy in contrast to the crispy fresh medu vada. Rich in protein and calcium from both lentil and dairy sources.
common_user_queries: dahi vada vs medu vada calories

confidence_reason: Exact match found in ICMR-NIN under entry EI000020 — Dahi Vada, standard preparation.
notes: Contains dairy (yogurt) and is not vegan or dairy-free. Cholesterol approximately 17mg per 2-piece serving (200g) from yogurt. Vitamin B12 present from dairy. Sugar content is elevated due to sweetened yogurt — request unsweetened for diabetic patients.
